rulings.Case Title: DAVID D CANON VS. NORRIS DAN WALTER DELANY AKA DAN W. DELANY AKA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Defendants Deposition And Production Of Documents, And Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for, Thursday, April 2, 2015, line 2. PLAINTIFF DAVID CANON's Motion to Compel Deposition and for Sanctions is GRANTED pursuant to CCP 2025.450(a). The Court imposes sanctions of $1,875 against Defendant. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for, Thursday, April 2, 2015, line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT VLAD COOD's Demurrer to First and Second Causes Of Action is OVERRULED as Cross-Complainant has pled sufficient facts to allege alter ego and breach of good faith and fair dealing. Demurrer to Third COA for defamation is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as the electronic communication is covered by the litigation privilege. Demurrer to Fourth COA for intentional infliction of emotional distress is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as Cross-Complainant has not pled sufficient facts to sustain the action for IIED. Demurrer to Fifth Cause Of Action is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as Cross-Complainant failed to address contractual notice requirements. Demurrer to Sixth and Seventh Causes Of Action is OVERRULED as Cross-Complainant has pled sufficient facts supporting the claims. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for, Thursday, April 2, 2015, line 3. CROSS DEFENDANTs' Motion to Strike Second Amended Cross Complaint is DENIED. The pleadings are not facially false and are supported by sufficient facts pled. There is no showing that the SAXC is a "sham" lacking truthful basis for any COA. (See Code Civ. Pro., § 436.) = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 2ND AMENDED CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for, Thursday, April 2, 2015, line 3. CROSS DEFENDANTs' Special Motion to Strike Second Amended Cross Complaintis DENIED. This action arises out of a private contract dispute and is not a public issue subject to anti-SLAPP. (Code Civ. Pro., § 425.16.) = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Discovery Pursuant To C.C.P. Sec. 425.16(G)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for, Thursday, April 2, 2015, line 3. CROSS COMPLAINANT KATIE HOM's Motion For Discovery Pursuant To C.C.P. Sec. 425.16(G) is OFF CALENDAR. Motion was untimely and Plaintiff was not diligent in seeking requested relief. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: PURE ENTERTAINMENT LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. SHUI KING KWON HOM et al
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for, Thursday, April 2, 2015, line 3. CROSS DEFENDANT PURE ENTERTAINMENT's Demurrer is SUSTAINED IN PART AND OVERRULED IN PART. Demurrer to Second Cause of Action is OVERRULED as Cross-Complainant has pled sufficient facts to show breach of good faith and fair dealing. Demurrer to Third COA for defamation is SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND as the electronic communication is covered by the litigation privilege. Demurrer to Fourth COA for intentional infliction of emotional distress is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as Cross-Complainant has not pled sufficient facts to sustain the action for IIED. Demurrer to Fifth Cause Of Action is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND as Cross-Complaint failed to address contractual notice requirements. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: BRIGID CRILLY, SUCCESSOR IN INTEREST TO MARIE FIRL VS. ANGELA THOMAS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt, And For Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for, Thursday, April 2, 2015, line 4. PLAINTIFF BRIGID CRILLY's Motion For Order To Show Cause Re: Contempt And For Sanctions is DENIED. Plaintiff failed to show that Defendant purposefully and in bad faith failed to obey the Court's June 27, 2014 order such that contempt sanctions should be issued. (See C.C.P. § 128.) = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: VERA CORT VS. CARL HILSZ **CONVERTED FROM U/D TO LIMITED CIVIL ACTION PER COURT ORDER SEP-29-11**
rulings.Calendar Matter: Amended Notice Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production And For A Monetary Sanction
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for, Thursday, April 2, 2015, line 5. PLAINTIFF VERA CORT's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Request For Production And For Monetary Sanctions. Hearing is required. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: FEL PROPERTIES B14 DE, LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED VS. PATRICK HAGEMEISTER REY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiffs Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Adjudication (Hearing Date 4.1.15)
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for, Thursday, April 2, 2015, line 8. PLAINTIFF's Motion for Summary Adjudication is GRANTED. Plaintiff has made a sufficient showing that the affirmative defenses are without merit. (Code Civ. Pro., § 437c(f)(1).)= (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MIRADOR VS. SHERYL NANBU ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Deft To Respond To Wtitten Disco
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for, Thursday, April 2, 2015, line 6. PLAINTIFF ROBERT MIRADOR's Motion to Compel Defendant to Respond to Written Discovery is OFF CALENDAR per request of the Moving Party. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO HOUSING AUTHORITY VS. SHERONDA PERKINS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for, Thursday, April 2, 2015, line 7. DEFENDANTs' Motion to Vacate Default and Default Judgment is GRANTED subject to opposition. Defendants moved promptly for default relief and made a satisfactory showing of mistake. (Code Civ. Pro., § 473(b).) Defendants shall post a bond in the sum of $7,616. = (501/REQ)
